mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This berry and yogurt combination appears to provide a balance of protein, fiber, and natural sugars. Portion awareness and choosing unsweetened yogurt can support more stable blood glucose responses.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A bowl of plain yogurt topped with fresh blueberries and red currants.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This meal appears to be a fish and avocado dish with sauce, which is generally lower in carbohydrates and higher in protein and fats. Consider the sauce quantity and any added sugars when estimating its effect on blood glucose.
mealAnalysis.meal_title: Tuna Tartare with Avocado and Roe
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A bowl of diced raw tuna served over mashed avocado and greens, topped with fish roe and dressed with a savory sauce.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This dish combines fresh fruit with a creamy topping and nuts, which can affect blood glucose differently. The fruit and any added sugars contribute carbohydrates, while the dairy and nuts add fat and protein that may slow glucose absorption. Consider portion size and how it fits into your overall carbohydrate goals.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A bowl of sliced fresh strawberries on a fruit granita or crushed ice base, topped with a dollop of creamy cheese or yogurt and sprinkled with crushed pistachios.

ingredients.name: Fruit granita or sweet crushed ice (likely with added sugar)

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This egg-based meal is relatively low in carbohydrates and higher in protein and fat. It may fit into a blood-glucose-conscious plan when portion size is considered and balanced with non-starchy vegetables or other fiber sources.
mealAnalysis.meal_title: Sous Vide Egg Bites with Uncured Bacon and Gouda
mealAnalysis.meal_description: Packaged sous vide egg bites made with cage-free eggs, uncured bacon, and gouda cheese, providing a high-protein, low-carbohydrate breakfast option.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This kiwi serving provides natural sugars with fiber and vitamin C; it can fit into a balanced eating pattern when portions are considered and combined with other nutrient-dense foods as needed.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: Two halves of a fresh green kiwi fruit with the skin on, served on a patterned tablecloth.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This appears to be a plain coffee, which is generally low in calories and carbohydrates. If any milk, cream, or sweetener is added, consider the portion and type used to manage overall carbohydrate and calorie intake.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A hot beverage served in a mug, likely plain black coffee or espresso with a light crema on top.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This slice of savory pie or quiche appears to include a refined-flour crust with cheese and vegetables, which can be higher in carbohydrates and fats; consider portion size and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables or lean protein if desired.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A slice of savory quiche with a baked pastry crust, egg and cheese filling, and toppings such as green bell peppers, red bell peppers, and mushrooms.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This serving of dark grapes provides natural sugars along with fiber and micronutrients. It may fit into a balanced meal when portions are measured and combined with protein or healthy fats.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A small bunch of fresh dark grapes served in a clear container.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This pizza appears to be high in refined carbohydrates and cheese, which may have a notable impact on blood glucose levels. Consider portion control, balancing it with non-starchy vegetables or protein during the day, and monitoring personal responses.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A thin-crust pizza topped with tomato sauce, cheese, red onions, spinach or other greens, and possible mushrooms, served on a metal tray with several slices remaining.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This cocktail appears to contain alcohol and added sugars, which may lead to a faster rise in blood glucose. It may be helpful to consider portion size, drink slowly, and pair with food higher in fiber or protein when choosing similar beverages.
mealAnalysis.meal_title: Creamy White Cocktail in Coupe Glass
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A pale, creamy white cocktail served in a coupe glass, garnished with a small yellow edible flower.

ingredients.name: Citrus juice (e.g., lemon or lime, if present)

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This salad appears to contain mixed vegetables with a creamy dressing and possibly cheese or another fat source, which may increase calories and fats; consider portion size and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables if needed.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A small bowl of salad containing lettuce, tomato pieces, and other chopped vegetables with a creamy dressing, possibly including cheese or boiled egg.

ingredients.name: Other mixed vegetables (e.g., cucumber or bell pepper)

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This apple provides natural carbohydrates along with fiber and can fit into a balanced eating plan when portions and overall carbohydrate intake for the day are considered.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A single fresh red apple served whole, with skin, on an outdoor table.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This appears to be a meat-topped flatbread or pizza-style dish that is relatively high in refined carbohydrates and fats. Pairing it with non-starchy vegetables, limiting portion size, and balancing it with protein-rich or higher-fiber foods in other meals can help manage its overall impact on blood sugar.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A round flatbread cut into slices, topped with a minced meat mixture and melted cheese, served on a plate with small side dishes visible in the background.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This salad appears to be mostly non-starchy vegetables with a light dressing, which generally has a modest impact on blood glucose. Be mindful of the amount and type of dressing used, as added sugars or large portions of oil can change its effect.
mealAnalysis.meal_title: Mixed Green Salad with Shredded Vegetables
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A small portion of mixed leafy greens with shredded carrots and cabbage or broccoli stems, lightly dressed and seasoned.

ingredients.name: Seasonings or seeds (e.g., pepper, herbs, possible seeds)

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This salad appears to be mostly non-starchy vegetables, which generally have a lower impact on blood glucose and can be included as part of a balanced meal. Pairing it with a source of protein and healthy fats can help make the meal more filling and balanced overall.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A plate of mixed leafy greens and lettuce topped with chopped fresh tomatoes and possibly a light dressing.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This peeled carrot is a non-starchy vegetable with fiber and moderate natural sugars, and it can fit into many meal plans when balanced with protein or healthy fats.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A single peeled raw carrot segment served on its own, likely to be eaten as a simple snack or side vegetable.

mealAnalysis.guidance_message: This meal appears to be primarily fish with a generous portion of sauerkraut, which is generally low in carbohydrates and may have a modest impact on blood glucose. Consider any added fats, sauces, or sides and individual responses to fermented foods when planning portions.
mealAnalysis.meal_description: A simple plate featuring a baked or steamed white fish fillet served alongside a large portion of sauerkraut on a blue dish.

ingredients.name: White fish fillet (e.g., cod or tilapia)
